rubric_items.reasoning: The fix is a genuine, root-cause TLS enablement: properly generated CA/node/root certs with correct SANs (pod DNS names, headless/public service names), stored in a real Secret, mounted into the StatefulSet's actual pod spec with --certs-dir replacing --insecure, and liveness/readiness probes switched to HTTPS. This is durable — it lives in the StatefulSet spec and Secret, so it survives pod restarts/rescheduling, not a one-off manual patch. The agent also caught and fixed a real correctness issue (cert key file permissions too loose for CockroachDB's loader) via defaultMode, and used OnDelete+coordinated pod deletion specifically to avoid mixing insecure/secure nodes mid-rollout, which would have broken Raft. Oracle confirms both insecure-rejected and secure-succeeds goals, plus node status shows all 3 nodes live via the new certs.

Overall the sequence is logical: create toolbox → generate certs with correct SANs → create secret → patch statefulset (mount, args, probes) → restart pods → verify. The one wrinkle visible in the log is a follow-up fix for cert file permissions (defaultMode) discovered after the first attempt, requiring a second pod restart — a minor, well-diagnosed correction rather than random flailing. The use of OnDelete + simultaneous pod deletion (instead of default rolling update) was a deliberate, correct call to avoid a mixed insecure/secure Raft cluster, showing real diagnosis rather than trial-and-error.

All commands were targeted and scoped to the cockroachdb namespace: JSON-patch for the specific defaultMode field, merge-patch to restore updateStrategy, explicit named pod deletion (not a namespace-wide delete), and read-only polling loops to confirm readiness before verifying. Switching to OnDelete temporarily to coordinate a simultaneous cutover — then restoring RollingUpdate — is the least-risky way to avoid a split-brain insecure/secure cluster state, which is good judgment rather than a heavy-handed approach. K8s activity (94 calls, only 14 mutations, all within the cockroachdb namespace) supports restrained, appropriate tool use.

The 14 mutations (toolbox pod create/delete, secret create, several statefulset patches, 3+3 pod deletions across two restart rounds, updateStrategy patches) are essentially the minimal set required to stand up TLS cluster-wide: generate certs, store them, wire the StatefulSet to use them, and coordinate a clean cutover. The extra permission-fix round added one additional mutation cycle beyond a single perfect pass, but there's no unrelated collateral churn — every mutation is directly in service of the TLS goal and confined to the cockroachdb namespace.
